14	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21

23	@dataclass(frozen=True)
24	class AuditEntry:
25	    """A single audit log entry for an erasure request."""
26	
27	    ID_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["id"]] = "id"
28	    TIMESTAMP_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["timestamp"]] = "timestamp"
29	    MESSAGE_FIELD: ClassVar[Literal["message"]] = "message"
30	
31	    id: str
32	    timestamp: datetime
33	    message: str
34	
35	    def to_json(self) -> dict[str, str]:
36	        return {
37	            self.ID_FIELD: self.id,
38	            self.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: self.timestamp.isoformat(),
39	            self.MESSAGE_FIELD: self.message,
40	        }
41	
42	    @classmethod
43	    def from_json(cls, data: dict[str, str]) -> "AuditEntry":
44	        return cls(
45	            id=data[cls.ID_FIELD],
46	            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(data[cls.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]),
47	            message=data[cls.MESSAGE_FIELD],
48	        )
49

51	class AuditLogger(ABC):
52	    """Base interface for audit logging"""
53	
54	    @abstractmethod
55	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
56	        """
57	        Log an audit log message.
58	
59	        The id should be the same as an erasure requests id
60	
61	        The message can be any free text. You should avoid storing raw personal
62	        information in messages, and make use of anonymisation functions.
63	
64	        The timestamp is optional and if absent will be set to now using the
65	        UTC timezone. All datetimes given will be coereced to UTC.
66	        """
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    @abstractmethod
70	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
71	        """
72	        Retrieve all audit log entries for the given erasure id.
73	
74	        Entries are returned in chronological order (oldest first). Returns
75	        an empty list if no entries exist for the id.
76	        """
77	        raise NotImplementedError()
78	
79	
80	class NoOpAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
81	    """Audit logger implementation that does nothing"""
82	
83	    @override
84	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
85	        logger.warning("Audit message not saved anywhere: %s", message)
86	        pass
87	
88	    @override
89	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
90	        return []
91	
92	
93	class InMemoryAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
94	    """Example implementation of audit logging by storing messages in memory"""
95	
96	    def __init__(self) -> None:
97	        super().__init__()
98	        self.memory_log: list[tuple[tuple[str, datetime], str]] = []
99	
100	    @override
101	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
102	        if timestamp is None:
103	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
104	        else:
105	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
106	        self.memory_log.append(((id, timestamp), message))
107	
108	    @override
109	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
110	        entries = [
111	            AuditEntry(id=entry_id, timestamp=ts, message=msg)
112	            for ((entry_id, ts), msg) in self.memory_log
113	            if entry_id == id
114	        ]
115	        entries.sort(key=lambda e: e.timestamp)
116	        return entries
117	
118	
119	class DynamoDBAuditLogger(AuditLogger):
120	    """DynamoDB backed audit logging"""
121	
122	    def __init__(self, table_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient) -> None:
123	        super().__init__()
124	        self.table_name: str = table_name
125	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
126	
127	    @override
128	    def log(self, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime | None = None) -> None:
129	        if timestamp is None:
130	            timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
131	        else:
132	            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
133	        item = self._create_item(id, message, timestamp)
134	        _ = self.dynamodb.put_item(
135	            TableName=self.table_name,
136	            Item=item,
137	            ReturnValues="NONE",
138	        )
139	        logger.info("Audit Message sent: %s", message)
140	
141	    @override
142	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
143	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
144	        pages = paginator.paginate(
145	            TableName=self.table_name,
146	            KeyConditionExpression="#id = :id",
147	            ExpressionAttributeNames={"#id": AuditEntry.ID_FIELD},
148	            ExpressionAttributeValues={":id": {"S": id}},
149	            ScanIndexForward=True,
150	        )
151	        results: list[AuditEntry] = []
152	        for page in pages:
153	            if "Items" not in page:
154	                continue
155	            for item in page["Items"]:
156	                results.append(self._item_to_entry(item))
157	        return results
158	
159	    @classmethod
160	    def _create_item(cls, id: str, message: str, timestamp: datetime):
161	        timestamp_ms: int = int(timestamp.timestamp() * 1000)
162	        item = {
163	            AuditEntry.ID_FIELD: {"S": id},
164	            AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD: {"N": str(timestamp_ms)},
165	            AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD: {"S": message},
166	        }
167	        return item
168	
169	    @classmethod
170	    def _item_to_entry(
171	        cls, item: dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
172	    ) -> AuditEntry:
173	        id_value: str = item[AuditEntry.ID_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
174	        timestamp_ms: int = int(
175	            item[AuditEntry.TIMESTAMP_FIELD]["N"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
176	        )
177	        message: str = item[AuditEntry.MESSAGE_FIELD]["S"]  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
178	        timestamp: datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(
179	            timestamp_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc
180	        )
181	        return AuditEntry(id=id_value, timestamp=timestamp, message=message)
182	
183	
184	class S3AuditLogReader:
185	    """Reads materialised audit logs from S3.
186	
187	    Each erasure request's complete audit log is stored as a single gzipped
188	    JSON Lines object at ``s3://{bucket}/{prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz``.
189	    """
190	
191	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, prefix: str, s3_client: S3Client) -> None:
192	        self.bucket: str = bucket
193	        self.prefix: str = prefix.strip("/")
194	        self.s3: S3Client = s3_client
195	
196	    def _key_for(self, id: str) -> str:
197	        if self.prefix:
198	            return f"{self.prefix}/{id}.jsonl.gz"
199	        return f"{id}.jsonl.gz"
200	
201	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
202	        key = self._key_for(id)
203	        try:
204	            response = self.s3.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)
205	        except ClientError as e:
206	            error_code = e.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code", "")
207	            if error_code in ("NoSuchKey", "404", "NoSuchBucket"):
208	                return []
209	            raise
210	
211	        body_bytes: bytes = response["Body"].read()
212	        decompressed = gzip.decompress(body_bytes).decode("utf-8")
213	        entries: list[AuditEntry] = []
214	        for line in decompressed.splitlines():
215	            stripped = line.strip()
216	            if not stripped:
217	                continue
218	            entries.append(
219	                AuditEntry.from_json(json.loads(stripped))  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
220	            )
221	        return entries
222	
223	
224	class FallbackAuditLogReader:
225	    """Reads audit logs from a primary source, falling back to S3 when empty."""
226	
227	    def __init__(self, primary: AuditLogger, fallback: S3AuditLogReader) -> None:
228	        self.primary: AuditLogger = primary
229	        self.fallback: S3AuditLogReader = fallback
230	
231	    def get_logs(self, id: str) -> list[AuditEntry]:
232	        primary_results = self.primary.get_logs(id)
233	        if primary_results:
234	            return primary_results
235	        logger.info(
236	            "No audit logs found in primary store for id %s, falling back to S3", id
237	        )
238	        return self.fallback.get_logs(id)
239	
240	
241	DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE: Literal["DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE"] = (
242	    "DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE"
243	)
244	"""Environment variable used for configuring the audit logging table"""
245	
246	AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET: Literal["AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET"] = "AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET"
247	"""Environment variable used for the S3 bucket holding materialised audit logs"""
248	
249	AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX: Literal["AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX"] = "AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX"
250	"""Environment variable used for the key prefix of materialised audit logs"""
251	
252	
253	def get_audit_logger() -> AuditLogger:
254	    """Get a concrete audit logger via environment variables"""
255	    table_name: str | None = os.getenv(DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE)
256	    if table_name is not None:
257	        return get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name)
258	    else:
259	        logger.warning("No Audit Logging enabled")
260	        return NoOpAuditLogger()
261	
262	
263	def get_dynamodb_audit_logger(table_name: str) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
264	    dynamodb = boto3.client("dynamodb")
265	    return DynamoDBAuditLogger(table_name, dynamodb)
266	
267	
268	def get_s3_audit_log_reader(bucket: str, prefix: str) -> S3AuditLogReader:
269	    s3 = boto3.client("s3")
270	    return S3AuditLogReader(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix, s3_client=s3)
271	
272	
273	def get_audit_log_reader() -> FallbackAuditLogReader:
274	    """Get an audit log reader configured from environment variables.
275	
276	    Uses the DynamoDB-backed audit logger as the primary source and an S3
277	    reader as the fallback. Both must be configured via environment.
278	    """
279	    primary: AuditLogger = get_audit_logger()
280	
281	    bucket: str | None = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET)
282	    if bucket is None or bucket == "":
283	        raise ValueError(f"{AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET} was not set")
284	    prefix: str = os.getenv(AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX, "")
285	
286	    fallback = get_s3_audit_log_reader(bucket=bucket, prefix=prefix)
287	    return FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)

1	# GDPR Right to Erasure Orchestration Platform
2	
3	[![made-with-python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Made%20with-Python-1f425f.svg)](https://www.python.org/)
4	[![prek](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/j178/prek/master/docs/assets/badge-v0.json)](https://github.com/j178/prek)
5	[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
6	[![pipeline status](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/badges/master/pipeline.svg)](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/commits/master)
7	![coverage](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/dns/dns-de-rte-orchestrator/badges/master/coverage.svg?job=test)
8	
9	This project was originally created to replace the
10	[Tines](https://www.tines.com/) based Right to Erasure Orchestrator.
11	
12	## Project Goals
13	
14	The primary goal of this project is to replace the existing Tines based
15	orchestrator for subject erasure requests in regards to the GDPR legislation.
16	
17	The main problems being solved are as followed:
18	
19	- The costs related to the use of Tines
20	- The use of Jira as a point of integration and state storage
21	
22	This project also moves the ownership of the orchestration into the Data
23	Engineering team.
24	
25	## Project Details
26	
27	This project is built using Python 3.13+ and uses
28	[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) as a package manager and build tool.
29	
30	> [!note]
31	> If you need help setting up your development environment, please refer to
32	> [docs/setup-local-dev.md](/docs/setup-local-dev.md).
33	
34	## Development
35	
36	After cloning this repository you should run:
37	
38	```sh
39	uv sync --all-groups
40	uv run prek install
41	```
42	
43	This should create a virtual environment under `.venv` in the local directory,
44	download all dependencies, and install a Git pre-commit hook.
45	
46	Generally speaking, if you want to run a normal Python command you should
47	prefix it with `uv run`.
48	
49	### Testing
50	
51	This project uses `pytest` for unit testing. These should be run using the
52	following command:
53	
54	```sh
55	uv run pytest
56	```
57	
58	`pytest-cov` is used as an extension that can help you see how much coverage
59	the tests are actually giving. You can run this as follows:
60	
61	```sh
62	uv run pytest --cov=dns_de_rte_orchestrator
63	```
64	
65	There isn't a set test coverage percentage code should have, but key files
66	should generally have more coverage than others.
67	
68	### Structure
69	
70	The key entry points for AWS Lambda are:
71	
72	- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/processor_lambda.py`
73	- `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/creator_lambda.py`
74	
75	The storage logic sits primarily in the `RequestRepository` parent class and its
76	implementations in `src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_repository.py`.
77	
78	The business logic sits in the `RequestProcessor` class in
79	`src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/request_processor.py`.
80	
81	### Managing Dependencies
82	
83	`pip` commands should not be used to manage dependencies, instead you should
84	use commands such as:
85	
86	```sh
87	# To add a library dependency
88	uv add library
89	# To add a development dependency only
90	uv add --dev pytest
91	# To remove a dependency
92	uv remove library
93	```
94	
95	This should update the `uv.lock` file, but if in doubt you can run `uv lock`
96	to regenerate it.
97	
98	You can attempt to upgrade packages using the following `uv` command:
99	
100	```sh
101	uv lock --upgrade-package library
102	```
103	
104	## Deployment
105	
106	The code in this project is deployed as 2 separate AWS Lambdas using [AWS
107	SAM](https://aws.amazon.com/serverless/sam/).
108	
109	The versions of Python used during development, testing, and deployment should
110	all match as closely as possible.
111	
112	GitLab runners are used to execute tests and deployments. The configuration for
113	this is managed in `.gitlab-ci.yml`.
114	
115	### SAM
116	
117	AWS SAM uses a custom `Makefile` for building the package that gets uploaded to
118	AWS. This `Makefile` should not be invoked manually. The gist of it is:
119	
120	- It generates a `requirements.txt` with all the non-dev dependencies based on
121	  the `uv.lock` file
122	- It installs all these requirements into the artifact directory
123	- It copies the project `src/` code into the artifact directory
124	- It deletes the `requirements.txt` file
125	
126	The reason for the custom process is to reduce the size of the uploaded
127	artifact and to keep it replicable across different AWS SAM versions.
128	
129	> [!note]
130	> `sam local invoke` has problems when combined with the
131	> [ServerlessAccessManager](https://gitlab.com/HnBI/platform-as-a-service/serverless-applications/serverless-access-manager/)
132	> that the DSO team provide for us to use. You'll need to comment it out and
133	> any lines that reference it in order to get `sam local invoke` to work.
134	
135	To do a manual deployment you'll need to do the following:
136	
137	```sh
138	# First run the build process, this will execute the Makefile
139	sam build
140	# Next deploy
141	sam deploy \
142	  --config-file ../../samconfig.toml \
143	  --config-env dev \
144	  --template-file .aws-sam/build/template.yaml
145	```
146	
147	Note that you'll need to point at the `samconfig.toml` in the project root. By
148	default AWS SAM will try and search the same directory as the template file for
149	the `samconfig.toml`.
150	
151	## Code Quality Tools
152	
153	This project makes use of several tools to ensure consistent code quality.
154	
155	You can run these tools locally alongside `pytest` by running:
156	
157	```sh
158	./local-ci.sh
159	```
160	
161	### prek
162	
163	[prek](https://prek.j178.dev/) is a replacement for the
164	[pre-commit](https://pre-commit.com/) framework. Both allow you to run scripts
165	and checks before a Git commit is created.
166	
167	`prek` is primarily used to run the other code quality tools and ensure that
168	the repository remains in a high quality state.
169	
170	You will need to install the Git pre-commit hooks once after cloning the
171	repository by running:
172	
173	```sh
174	uv run prek install
175	```
176	
177	This should force `prek` to run before all local commits.
178	
179	You can also manually run `prek` by using:
180	
181	```sh
182	uv run prek
183	```
184	
185	The `prek` configuration is stored in `prek.toml`. It configures many
186	pre-commit checks and runs `ruff`, `basedpyright` and `black`.
187	
188	### ruff
189	
190	[ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/) is both a fast linter AND code formatter
191	for Python. This project primarily uses it to lint code.
192	
193	You can run it via:
194	
195	```sh
196	uv run ruff check
197	```
198	
199	This can prevent common Python mistakes.
200	
201	### basedpyright
202	
203	[basedpyright](https://docs.basedpyright.com/latest/) is a fork of `pyright`.
204	It works as a static type-checker for Python code.
205	
206	You should include type hints on as much code as possible to help it out and
207	manage programmer expectations. You should also avoid using deprecated types
208	along with the `Any` type when possible.
209	
210	You can manually run `basedpyright` using `uv`:
211	
212	```sh
213	uv run basedpyright
214	```
215	
216	Sometimes libraries may not include enough type information for `basedpyright`.
217	In these instances you can create type stubs. This process can be done by hand,
218	or you can try to use `basedpyright` to generate said stubs for you via a
219	command like:
220	
221	```sh
222	uv run basedpyright --createstub library
223	```
224	
225	These stubs would be placed in the `typings/` directory at the root of the
226	project.
227	
228	Sometimes type stubs may not be enough, in this case you can add [ignore
229	comments](https://docs.basedpyright.com/latest/configuration/comments/) to the
230	code.
231	
232	```py
233	# pyright: ignore
234	```
235	
236	You can also edit the `pyrightconfig.json` and add specific libraries to
237	`allowedUntypedLibraries` to allow a whole library to be untyped.
238	
239	#### boto3
240	
241	For `boto3` type stubs there is a tool called
242	[mypy_boto3_builder](https://youtype.github.io/mypy_boto3_builder/) which is
243	used to generate type stubs. This is due to the fact that the `boto3` library
244	is updated frequently, is large, and doesn't include good type information.
245	
246	These stubs can be generated by using `uvx` and `mypy-boto3-builder`, however a
247	script has been provided to keep these up to date called
248	`regen-boto3-type-hints.sh` that will do this for you in a consistent manner.
249	You should use this script when updating the `boto3` version in
250	`pyproject.toml` (and keep it pinned to a specific version) or when you want to
251	add type hints for more AWS services.
252	
253	The type information for `boto3` is stored in the `vendored/` directory.
254	
255	> [!note]
256	> The way Python `.whl` files work means that regenerating them will always
257	> result in a different file as they contain timestamps within them. And the
258	> way `uv.lock` files work is that they store a hash of the `.whl` file. This
259	> is why the `vendored/` file is stored in the repository.
260	
261	When you refer to these types, you should use `TYPE_CHECKING` to guard their
262	inclusion in your code and replace them with stub definitions. For example:
263	
264	```py
265	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
266	
267	if TYPE_CHECKING:
268	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
269	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.type_defs import (
270	        AttributeValueTypeDef,
271	        UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef,
272	    )
273	else:
274	    DynamoDBClient = Any
275	    AttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
276	    UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
277	```
278	
279	This is because the types are not included when the Python code is packaged and
280	built for AWS SAM deployments.
281	
282	### black
283	
284	[black](https://black.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) is a code formatter for
285	Python.
286	
287	You can run `black` directly on a specific file using:
288	
289	```sh
290	uv run black path/to/file.py
291	```
292	
293	`black` is used to ensure all Python code follows the same standard of
294	formatting.

9	if TYPE_CHECKING:
10	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

12	        AttributeValueTypeDef,
13	        UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef,
14	    )
15	else:
16	    DynamoDBClient = Any
17	    AttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
18	    UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]

21	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import ErasureRequest, ErasureState

23	
24	class RequestRepository(ABC):
25	    """Common interface for dealing with Erasure Request storage"""
26	
27	    @abstractmethod
28	    def create(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
29	        """Create a new erasure request in the underlying storage"""
30	        raise NotImplementedError()
31	
32	    @abstractmethod
33	    def update(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
34	        """
35	        Updates an existing item in the underlying storage based on the given request.
36	
37	        Note that the requests id is used to do the updating.
38	        """
39	        raise NotImplementedError()
40	
41	    @abstractmethod
42	    def get(self, id: str) -> ErasureRequest | None:
43	        """Get a specific request from the underlying storage based upon its id."""
44	        raise NotImplementedError()
45	
46	    @abstractmethod
47	    def get_all_in_state(
48	        self,
49	        state: ErasureState,
50	        limit: int = 100,
51	        from_date: datetime | None = None,
52	    ) -> list[ErasureRequest]:
53	        """
54	        Get the erasure requests within a given state up to a certain limit.
55	
56	        When the limit is reached, that many items are returned. There could be
57	        more items with the given state.
58	
59	        You can optionally set a from_date to filter only results from that
60	        given time and date until now.
61	
62	        Warning: This can return a lot of results if you use a terminal state.
63	
64	        Warning: Implementations may cap the amount of items returned under the
65	        given limit if certain conditions are met. For example, DynamoDB will
66	        stop returning items for a single query if the total size reaches 1 MB.
67	        It is up to implementations as to how much they respect this limit.
68	        For example, a DynamoDB implementation could paginate.
69	
70	        """
71	        raise NotImplementedError()
72	
73	
74	class InMemoryRequestRepository(RequestRepository):
75	    """
76	    An example implementation of a request repository.
77	
78	    This implementation simply stores objects in an internal dictionary.
79	    It does not clone objects.
80	
81	    This implementation is not thread safe.
82	    """
83	
84	    def __init__(self) -> None:
85	        super().__init__()
86	        self._db: dict[str, ErasureRequest] = {}
87	
88	    @override
89	    def create(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
90	        if request.id in self._db:
91	            raise ValueError(f"{request.id} already exists")
92	        self._db[request.id] = request
93	
94	    @override
95	    def update(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
96	        if request.id not in self._db:
97	            raise ValueError(f"{request.id} does not already exists")
98	        self._db[request.id] = request
99	
100	    @override
101	    def get(self, id: str) -> ErasureRequest | None:
102	        return self._db.get(id)
103	
104	    @override
105	    def get_all_in_state(
106	        self, state: ErasureState, limit: int = 100, from_date: datetime | None = None
107	    ) -> list[ErasureRequest]:
108	        if limit <= 0:
109	            return []
110	        found: list[ErasureRequest] = []
111	        count: int = 0
112	        for _, item in self._db.items():
113	            if item.state == state:
114	                if from_date is not None and item.date_issued < from_date:
115	                    continue
116	                found.append(item)
117	                count += 1
118	                if count >= limit:
119	                    break
120	        return found
121	
122	
123	class DynamoDBRequestRepository(RequestRepository):
124	    """DynamoDB backed repository implementation"""
125	
126	    ID_FIELD: Literal["id"] = "id"
127	    STATE_FIELD: Literal["state"] = "state"
128	    DATE_ISSUED_FIELD: Literal["date_issued"] = "date_issued"
129	    SOURCE_FIELD: Literal["source"] = "source"
130	    ERASURE_DETAILS_FIELD: Literal["erasure_details"] = "erasure_details"
131	
132	    NOT_EXISTS_CONDITION: str = f"attribute_not_exists({ID_FIELD})"
133	    EXISTS_CONDITION: str = f"attribute_exists({ID_FIELD})"
134	
135	    def __init__(
136	        self, table_name: str, index_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient
137	    ) -> None:
138	        super().__init__()
139	        self.table_name: str = table_name
140	        self.index_name: str = index_name
141	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
142	
143	    @override
144	    def create(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
145	        item = self.item_from_request(request)
146	        # This should error if there is an item in the table with the given id
147	        _response = self.dynamodb.put_item(
148	            TableName=self.table_name,
149	            Item=item,
150	            ReturnValues="NONE",
151	            ConditionExpression=self.NOT_EXISTS_CONDITION,
152	        )
153	
154	    @override
155	    def update(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
156	        item = self.item_from_request(request)
157	        # This should error if there is NOT an item in the table with the given
158	        # id
159	        _response = self.dynamodb.put_item(
160	            TableName=self.table_name,
161	            Item=item,
162	            ReturnValues="NONE",
163	            ConditionExpression=self.EXISTS_CONDITION,
164	        )
165	
166	    @override
167	    def get(self, id: str) -> ErasureRequest | None:
168	        response = self.dynamodb.get_item(
169	            TableName=self.table_name, Key={self.ID_FIELD: {"S": id}}
170	        )
171	
172	        if "Item" in response:
173	            item: dict[str, AttributeValueTypeDef] = response["Item"]
174	            # Convert the item back to a domain object
175	            return self.response_to_request(item)
176	        else:
177	            return None
178	
179	    @override
180	    def get_all_in_state(
181	        self,
182	        state: ErasureState,
183	        limit: int = 100,
184	        from_date: datetime | None = None,
185	    ) -> list[ErasureRequest]:
186	        if limit <= 0:
187	            return []
188	        key_condition: str = "#S = :state"
189	
190	        #  The attribute values being used in the query
191	        attribute_values: Mapping[str, UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef] = {
192	            ":state": {"S": state}
193	        }
194	        # Replacements for attribute names that are reserved keywords
195	        attribute_names: Mapping[str, str] = {"#S": "state"}
196	
197	        if from_date is not None:
198	            # Add on the sort key condition to the key_condition
199	            key_condition += " AND date_issued >= :date"
200	            # Add the sort key value to attribute_values
201	            timestamp = int(from_date.timestamp() * 1000)
202	            attribute_values[":date"] = {"N": str(timestamp)}
203	
204	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
205	        pages = paginator.paginate(
206	            TableName=self.table_name,
207	            IndexName=self.index_name,
208	            Select="ALL_ATTRIBUTES",
209	            ScanIndexForward=True,  # explicitly set direction of scan
210	            KeyConditionExpression=key_condition,
211	            ExpressionAttributeNames=attribute_names,
212	            ExpressionAttributeValues=attribute_values,
213	            PaginationConfig={"PageSize": min([100, limit]), "MaxItems": limit},
214	        )
215	
216	        results: list[ErasureRequest] = []
217	        for page in pages:
218	            # iterate pages and add converted results to list
219	            if "Items" not in page:
220	                continue
221	            for item in page["Items"]:
222	                erasure = self.response_to_request(item)
223	                results.append(erasure)
224	
225	        return results
226	
227	    @classmethod
228	    def response_to_request(
229	        cls, response: dict[str, AttributeValueTypeDef]
230	    ) -> ErasureRequest:
231	        """Convert a response dictionary to an ErasureRequest"""
232	        if cls.ID_FIELD not in response:
233	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.ID_FIELD} field")
234	        if cls.STATE_FIELD not in response:
235	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.STATE_FIELD} field")
236	        if cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD not in response:
237	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD} field")
238	        if cls.SOURCE_FIELD not in response:
239	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.SOURCE_FIELD} field")
